title: Architecture Bridge Declaration
description: A bridge point where information crosses a CORE layer boundary. Declared constitutionally to make inter-layer data flow queryable without grepping the source tree.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this bridge point.

description.description: What crosses this bridge and why it matters architecturally.

bridge_class.description: The Python class or module that performs the layer crossing.

bridge_layer.description: The CORE layer where the bridge class lives (will, body, api, cli, shared).

layer.description: Origin layer (mind, body, will, api, shared, database).

context.description: Human-readable description of where the data comes from.

consuming_types.description: Named types or table rows the bridge reads from its source.

via.description: The API or method used to write (e.g. Worker.post_finding).

mechanism.description: How ownership of the sink record is attributed (e.g. worker_uuid, proposal_id).

field.description: The database column or payload key that carries attribution.

authority_adrs.description: ADR IDs whose decisions govern this bridge's design.
